title: Vets Must Show Prices and Cap Prescription Fees to Help Pet Owners
summary: In the UK, a watchdog called the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has made new rules for vets to help pet owners. Vets must now publish their price lists so people can see how much services cost. Also, fees for written prescriptions, which let pet owners buy medicine elsewhere, will be capped at £21 for the first medicine and £12.50 for more. This is because prices have been rising fast, and many pet owners didn't know they could save money by shopping around. The CMA found that over 70% of people buy medicine from their vet, but they could save £200 a year or more by looking online. Vets will also have to tell owners if they are part of a large group, so it's clearer who owns the practice. These changes aim to make it easier for pet owners to choose the best care for their pets without surprises. The rules will start later this year, and the British Veterinary Association says they will help people make better choices.

background_read: In the UK, many families have pets, and taking care of them can be expensive. Vets are doctors for animals, and they charge for services like check-ups and medicine. Sometimes, pet owners don't know all the costs or options, which can make it hard to choose the best care. The CMA is a group that looks at businesses to make sure they are fair and competitive. They found that vet prices were going up quickly, and people could save money by comparing prices or buying medicine online with a prescription. These new rules aim to make things clearer and cheaper for pet owners, so they can give their pets the care they need without worrying too much about money.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) and vets in the UK
WHAT: New rules requiring vets to publish price lists and cap prescription fees at £21 for the first medicine and £12.50 for more
WHY: To help pet owners make informed choices and save money as prices have been rising fast

perspectives.description: The CMA and British Veterinary Association believe these rules will help pet owners by making prices clear and reducing costs, allowing better care for pets.
